source: KVTX script vectors and shared common prototype distilled into a shared-ownership readiness manifest
description: Pins the KVTX script behavior families that must remain covered before KVTX script parsing and writing delegates to shared code.

expected.commonDecision: KVTX shared ownership remains valid while every vector named by this readiness manifest is executable from shared commonTest, Android and iOS KVTX tests continue to reference the same vectors, write-and-commit framing, empty data writes, unsigned 32-bit keys, multiple-key selection, append/remove behavior, EX zero-index behavior, non-empty EX index ignore policy, unknown-command stop policy, malformed-script typed error policy, truncated payload platform vectors, and the shared tests are compile-verified.

notes: This readiness manifest is a shared ownership gate for KVTX script behavior-family completeness only; platform compatibility for truncated write payloads remains explicit until shared parsing chooses one typed malformed-script policy and adapters preserve any required public behavior.
